trust_note.description: Review-trust consequence when workspace orientation is skipped for planned or high-risk work.

orientation.description: Minimum workspace orientation guidance for the implementer.

rule.description: Boundary for what implement --changed can derive from available workspace state.

items.description: One class of guidance the command can infer.

can_infer.description: Information classes derived from paths, config, active planning, or package metadata.

items.description: One class of guidance the command cannot infer.

cannot_infer.description: Information classes that still require human or agent judgment.

when_uncertain.description: Routing instruction when changed-path context is insufficient.

kind.description: Discriminator for the execution posture payload.

capability_posture.description: Task capability posture inferred from changed paths and optional task text.

runtime_resolution.description: Compact stay-local, stronger-reasoning, external-delegation, or manual-handoff recommendation.

delegation_control.description: Local human-control mode and whether automatic delegation is permitted.

selected_target.description: Suggested local delegation target when one matches the inferred posture.

capability_handoff_packets.description: Capability-aware handoff packet templates for escalation, down-routing, human clarification, review fallback, and no-safe-route cases.

recommended_action.description: Recommended execution category for the implementer.

quality_tradeoff.description: Why the recommendation preserves or improves implementation quality.

token_tradeoff.description: When token saving is acceptable or should be deferred.

ready_handoff.description: Prompt or handoff packet to use when local mode allows manual or suggested delegation.

delegation_decision.description: Compact local delegation, escalation, clarification, or stay-local decision derived from local posture and changed-path signals.

implementation_allowed.description: Whether the selected assignment/delegation posture permits local implementation for this scope.

assignment_policy.description: Structured assignment policy and manual transport posture used for the delegation decision.

target_evidence.description: Current target evidence used to evaluate assignment candidates.

assignment_decision.description: Current assignment decision derived from policy, candidates, and target evidence.

assignment_gate.description: Assignment gate preserving the handoff identity, allowed effects, proof obligation, and mutation baseline.

delegated_run_lifecycle.description: Delegated run lifecycle, public operations, transport status, and return admission gate.

decomposition_delegation.description: Delegation candidates discovered from active planning decompositions, when active decomposed work is visible.

target_evidence.description: Provenance-aware target suitability evidence for the current changed-path scope.

assignment_policy.description: Configured best-fit assignment policy for the current target and task scope.

assignment_decision.description: Derived assignment or escalation decision based on target evidence and policy.

assignment_gate.description: Implementation gate that blocks unresolved or mismatched required-best-fit assignments.

delegated_run_lifecycle.description: Delegated-run lifecycle projection for assignment handoff, execution, return, and admission.

implementation_allowed.description: Whether the current implement posture may proceed under assignment and safety gates.

items.description: One limit of the inferred execution posture.

inference_limits.description: Limits that prevent the posture recommendation from becoming hidden execution authority.

execution_posture.description: Actionable local execution posture for orchestration before implementation.

target_evidence.description: Provenance-aware target suitability evidence for the current changed-path scope.

assignment_policy.description: Configured best-fit assignment policy for the current target and task scope.

assignment_decision.description: Derived assignment or escalation decision based on target evidence and policy.

assignment_gate.description: Implementation gate that blocks unresolved or mismatched required-best-fit assignments.

delegated_run_lifecycle.description: Delegated-run lifecycle projection for assignment handoff, execution, return, and admission.

implementation_allowed.description: Whether the current implement posture may proceed under assignment and safety gates.

durable_intent.description: Compact durable system or subsystem intent pressure that may affect scope, proof, delegation, or closeout.

kind.description: Discriminator for the parent/original-intent status packet.

status.description: Whether the recorded parent/original intent is satisfied, open, or only guidance.

parent_intent_status.description: Parent/original-intent status preserving larger intent across bounded slices.

kind.description: Discriminator for the applicable-intent status packet.

status.description: Whether applicable-intent evidence is present, guidance-only, or requires attention.

applicable_intent_status.description: Applicable-intent evidence, conflicts, missing authority, and manual-verification obligations.

durable_intent_promotion.description: Guidance for promoting non-finishable task intent into durable memory, docs, subsystem intent, or system intent.

delegation_decision.description: Top-level copy of the compact delegation decision for next-action consumers.

workflow_sufficiency.description: Small decision record that says whether the current implementer packet is enough for the next implementation step or which exact detail field should be requested.

planning_safety_gate.description: Planning ownership guard for broad, high-assurance, decomposed, or scope-widened implementation work.

continuation_state.description: Compact continuation-state contract for preserving only unfinished or handoff-relevant work, not historical residue.

authority_hierarchy.description: Authority and promotion-path guidance for deciding which repo surfaces are current instruction, durable memory, future work, or historical audit.

compliance_economics.description: Boundary statement for what Agentic Workspace can enforce directly and where it instead makes noncompliance visible or costly.

kind.description: Discriminator for the acceptance reconciliation guidance.

status.description: Whether acceptance reconciliation is required before closeout for the current context.

rule.description: Why validation alone is not enough for closeout.

items.description: One explicit symbol or outcome extracted from the task text.

requested_outcomes.description: Concrete task outcomes available for acceptance mapping.

items.description: One inferred acceptance item to reconcile before closeout.

acceptance_items.description: Definition-of-done items inferred from task intent.

acceptance_item_count.description: Number of acceptance items to reconcile before closeout.

acceptance_closeout_rule.description: Acceptance-specific closeout rule for claiming done.

items.description: One required acceptance reconciliation check.

checklist.description: Checklist for mapping requested outcomes to delivered behavior and proof.

compact_closeout_prompt.description: Short prompt agents can use before final answer or lane closeout.

task_text_available.description: Whether the CLI invocation included task text to reconcile.

task_carry_forward_hint.description: Short reminder to preserve task text across start and implement calls.

acceptance_reconciliation.description: Closeout guard against passing self-authored tests while missing requested behavior.

kind.description: Discriminator for the lightweight objective-drift heuristic.

status.description: Whether requested task terms appear missing from changed surfaces.

reason.description: Why no drift comparison could be made, when applicable.

items.description: One requested symbol or concrete outcome extracted from the task text.

requested_outcomes.description: Task-specific terms checked against changed files.

acceptance_item_count.description: Number of inferred acceptance items that should be reconciled before closeout.

acceptance_closeout_rule.description: Acceptance-specific closeout rule carried into objective-drift guidance.

items.description: Requested outcome not found in the changed file contents.

missing_from_changed_surface.description: Requested terms that were not observed in changed files.

items.description: Requested outcome the agent explicitly reconciled as removed or retired.

removed_or_retired_outcomes.description: Reserved for explicit reconciliation evidence; AW does not infer removal or retirement from prompt keyword markers.

force.description: Whether this objective-drift signal is advisory or required before a completion claim.

allowed_now.description: Action that remains allowed while the objective-drift signal is unresolved.

items.description: One claim or action blocked until the missing outcome is reconciled.

blocked_until_reconciled.description: Claims or actions that the drift signal limits.

claim_boundary.description: Completion claim boundary created by the objective-drift signal.

resolution_selector.description: Selector or compact field that resolves or explains the signal.

action_effect.description: Action-changing semantics for the objective-drift caution signal.

rule.description: Closeout rule agents should apply when drift is suspected.

recommended_next_action.description: Small next step for resolving or dismissing the drift signal.

heuristic.description: Brief explanation of the comparison method.

items.description: Objective-drift judgment the agent owns.

agent_owned_decisions.description: Semantic acceptance decisions AW must not infer from prompt keyword markers.

objective_drift.description: Heuristic warning when changed files do not mention explicit requested outcomes.

items.description: One entry in the before handoff list.

before_handoff.description: Ordered before handoff entries used by this contract.

items.description: One entry in the stop when list.

stop_when.description: Ordered stop when entries used by this contract.

items.description: One field path or identity that must survive handoff and closeout.

must_preserve.description: Decision identities and claim-boundary fields that downstream consumers must preserve.

handoff_requirements.description: Information that must be preserved before pausing or handing off work.

next_allowed_action.description: Smallest safe implementation action after reading this context.

memory_decision_packet.description: Command-backed Memory pull/capture decision packet surfaced during implementation.

kind.description: Discriminator for the Memory decision packet.

stage.description: Workflow stage that produced the packet.

force.description: Whether Memory pull/capture is not applicable, recommended, or required before a claim.

why_visible.description: Short reason this packet is visible to the agent.

pull.description: Memory pull status, route command, route candidates, and read budget.

capture.description: Durable-learning capture status, candidate owner surfaces, and capture commands.

authority_boundary.description: Boundary separating what AW observes and recommends from agent and human decisions.

items.description: One guardrail or limitation for interpreting the packet.

limits.description: Guardrails preventing keyword-triggered policy, hidden writes, or bulk-read diligence claims.

kind.description: Discriminator for the normalized operating-loop decision packet.

closeout_state.description: Structural closeout state derived from Memory, Planning, and Verification facts.

safe_claim.description: Maximum structurally safe completion claim class for this output.

residue_owner.description: Primary owner surface for unresolved residue or blockers.

items.description: One required action code before a full closure claim.

required_before_full_closure.description: Structured action codes required before full closure is structurally safe.

memory.description: Memory pull and capture state projected from the Memory decision packet.

state.description: Whether relevant Memory was pulled, structurally dismissed, or not applicable.

reason_code.description: Structured reason for the Memory pull state.

route_ref.description: Selector or route reference for pulled Memory, when available.

capture.description: Whether reusable learning capture is absent, recommended, or required.

planning.description: Planning state relevant to full closure safety.

state.description: Active, continuation, closeout-required, or absent Planning state.

plan_ref.description: Planning record or execplan reference, when available.

blocks_full_closure.description: Whether the Planning state structurally blocks an unqualified full closure claim.

verification.description: Verification or proof state relevant to the current claim boundary.

state.description: Selected proof status used to decide structural closure safety.

proof_ref.description: Proof command, receipt, or source reference, when available.

blocks_full_closure.description: Whether the proof state structurally blocks an unqualified full closure claim.

reasons.description: Short structured reason codes explaining blockers or residue.

code.description: Structured blocker or residue reason code.

owner.description: Owner surface responsible for resolving the reason.

ref.description: Optional reference to the proof, plan, route, or owner surface.

kind.description: Discriminator for dynamic task posture.

operating_posture.description: Resolved optimization, artifact, initiative, assurance, and delegation posture for this task.

workflow_obligations.description: Matched workflow obligations with stage, force, scope, and provenance.

improvement_obligations.description: Active improvement-pressure obligations that affect proof, closeout, allowed actions, or posture adherence.

skill_routes.description: Task-selected skills, prompts, or routing fragments.

allowed_actions.description: Actions allowed under the resolved posture.

forbidden_actions.description: Actions forbidden under the resolved posture.

proof_boundaries.description: Proof boundaries selected for the current task.

closeout_boundaries.description: Closeout and claim boundaries selected for the current task.

read_budget.description: Read-budget profile and selector/deep-read limits for this task.

authority_boundaries.description: Authority boundaries with source and owner provenance.

knowledge_gates.description: Compact pre-work knowledge gates selected when routed knowledge can change task interpretation, work shape, proof, allowed action, or completion claims.

gate_summary.description: Count and force summary for selected knowledge gates.

blocked_actions.description: Concrete actions blocked by selected knowledge gates.

next_allowed_action.description: Smallest safe next action after applying knowledge gates.

output_shape_requirements.description: Output-shape constraints selected for this task.

review_rubrics.description: Review rubrics selected for this task.

module_contributions.description: Matched module posture contributions with trigger and projection provenance.

provenance.description: Config, obligation, module, or command sources used to assemble this packet.

dynamic_instruction_projection.description: Compact dynamic AGENTS.md-style projection assembled for this task instead of static prose expansion.

posture_adherence.description: Closeout/report visibility for whether the selected posture was followed or requires explanation.

task_posture_packet.description: Task-specific posture and dynamic instruction packet selected from config, task facts, workflow obligations, and module declarations.

kind.description: Discriminator for changed-path proof selection.

items.description: Changed path used for proof selection.

changed_paths.description: Paths used to select validation lanes.

items.description: Condition that activates this proof lane.

when.description: Path or task conditions that activate this lane.

items.description: Required validation command for this lane.

recovery_signal.description: What to inspect or route when this proof lane is insufficient.

items.description: Selected proof lane.

selected_lanes.description: Proof lanes selected by changed paths.

items.description: Required command selected from proof lanes.
Optional command that can strengthen proof.

generated_cli_freshness.description: Generated CLI freshness signal for changed paths that affect generated command packages.

status.description: Whether the freshness check is required or advisory for the current changed paths.

freshness_check_command.description: Check command that detects stale generated CLI outputs without regenerating them.

refresh_command.description: Command to refresh generated CLI outputs when the freshness check reports staleness.

validation_command.description: Selected validation command that proves generated command package freshness.

obligation.description: Whether the named check is required or advisory.

proof_obligations.description: Structured proof contract separating required proof gates, recommended confidence checks, and agent-selected extra validation.

kind.description: Discriminator for the proof-obligations packet.

required_proof.description: Required proof gate for completion claims.

recommended_confidence_checks.description: Optional checks that may raise confidence but do not replace required proof.

agent_selected_extra_validation.description: Validation the agent chooses when task risk or failures warrant more evidence.

completion_claim_rule.description: Rule that blocks completion claims until required proof is satisfied.

compatibility.description: Compatibility notes for existing proof command list consumers.

validation_plan.description: Executable validation plan derived from selected lanes.

items.description: Condition that requires broader proof.

broaden_when.description: Signals that narrow proof is no longer enough.

items.description: Condition that requires escalation or stronger review.

escalate_when.description: Signals that the implementer should not guess.

acceptance_guidance.description: Guidance that proof should demonstrate acceptance satisfaction, not only command success.

proof_selection.description: Changed-path proof selection and validation plan.

kind.description: Discriminator for the task-intent acceptance payload.

status.description: Whether acceptance was inferred from task text or is unavailable.

source.description: Source used to infer the acceptance checklist.

confidence.description: Confidence in the inferred definition of done.

expectation.description: Definition-of-done expectation to satisfy or explicitly route.

proof_hint.description: Compact hint for selecting proof that demonstrates this expectation.

status.description: Current reconciliation state for this acceptance item.

source.description: Source signal that produced this acceptance item.

items.description: One inferred acceptance expectation.

closeout_required.description: Whether closeout must reconcile acceptance before claiming done.

proof_rule.description: Rule for selecting proof that demonstrates acceptance satisfaction.

unresolved_questions.description: Questions that block honest acceptance reconciliation.

status.description: Whether validation is ready to inspect or run.

status.description: Whether capability posture was inferred, configured, or unavailable.

posture.description: Human-readable posture details used for review.

items.description: Risk flag that affects capability routing.

risk_flags.description: Risk signals inferred from paths or task text.

items.description: Evidence an implementer should inspect before execution.

inspection_evidence_required.description: Context required before trusting the posture.
